high_value_elements: Correctly identifies electroacupuncture frequency-dependent mediator release: low frequency (~2 Hz) stimulates enkephalin and beta-endorphin release at spinal and supraspinal levels; high frequency (~100 Hz) stimulates dynorphin release primarily at spinal cord level
Correctly identifies PBM mechanism: photons in 635-904 nm range absorbed by cytochrome C oxidase in mitochondria, producing ATP, nitric oxide, and reactive oxygen species

Correctly identifies adipokine mechanism of obesity amplifying OA pain: excess adipose tissue produces pro-inflammatory adipokines (leptin dysregulation, reduced adiponectin) that upregulate inflammatory cytokines in joint tissue independent of mechanical load
Identifies therapeutic exercise as reducing pain through muscle strengthening (joint stability), endorphin release, and improved proprioception — not merely mechanical load redistribution
major_concerns: Cannot distinguish electroacupuncture from dry needling or explain the frequency-dependent mediator difference
Cannot explain PBM mechanism beyond 'it heats the tissue'
Cannot explain why weight loss benefits OA pain beyond mechanical load reduction

high_value_elements: Applies COAST staging to the 8-year-old Labrador: bilateral radiographic OA with functional impairment requires multimodal approach
Recommends weight loss as first-line co-primary intervention with realistic target (BCS 5/9) and adipokine mechanism of benefit
Identifies force plate gait analysis as an objective non-owner-reported outcome measure to quantify treatment response
For cats: correctly recommends environmental modifications including steps/ramps to elevated resting spots, lowered litter box walls, food and water bowls at accessible height, soft warm bedding
Identifies escalation threshold: persistent pain scale scores above treatment trigger, or inability to perform baseline activities despite 4-8 weeks consistent non-pharmacological management
major_concerns: Cannot apply COAST staging or explain what it guides
Recommends non-pharmacological management as sole long-term approach without identifying any escalation threshold
Has no objective outcome measure for treatment response

high_value_elements: Identifies active neoplasia as an absolute contraindication to PBM: confirmed splenic mass requires withholding laser therapy due to risk of stimulating tumor growth

Correctly applies escalation threshold: if non-pharmacological management consistently implemented for 4-8 weeks without reaching target pain score or functional improvement, pharmacological addition is indicated
Correctly identifies Synovetin OA (Tn-117, intra-articular radiopharmaceutical) as an emerging modality for canine elbow OA with ~1-year duration from a single injection
major_concerns: Applies PBM to a patient with confirmed neoplasia without raising the contraindication
Cannot identify any clinical threshold at which non-pharmacological management alone becomes a standard-of-care failure

high_value_elements: Explains why PBM is contraindicated over neoplasia: photobiomodulation promotes cell proliferation and angiogenesis; same mechanisms that promote tissue healing may stimulate tumor growth
Explains force plate rationale: owner-reported assessments are subjective and prone to accommodation bias; force plate measures weight-bearing symmetry objectively
Justifies electroacupuncture over dry needling for IVDD and neuropathic conditions based on spinal cord-level dynorphin release at high frequencies providing segmental inhibitory effect
major_concerns: Cannot explain why any non-pharmacological modality is contraindicated in specific patient types
Cannot justify technique selection over alternatives with mechanism-based reasoning

high_value_elements: Acknowledges evidence quality for many non-pharmacological modalities in veterinary medicine is lower than ideal; can distinguish well-supported uses from extrapolations
Identifies credentialing requirement for acupuncture and electroacupuncture in veterinary practice
Acknowledges exotic species have minimal species-validated non-pharmacological evidence and describes careful monitoring approach
major_concerns: Presents non-pharmacological modalities as universally safe and applicable to all species without evidence qualification
Cannot identify any training or credentialing requirement for acupuncture or electroacupuncture

high_value_elements: Integrates non-pharmacological modalities into a coherent multimodal plan where each component targets a different mechanism: weight management (adipokine reduction), exercise (muscle stabilization, endorphin), PBM (mitochondrial/anti-inflammatory), EA (endogenous opioid), environmental modification (load/stress reduction)
Applies COAST staging framework to guide which modalities are appropriate at each disease stage
Connects non-pharmacological content to FAS and stress reduction: environmental modification in cats reduces both pain perception and stress-amplified pain
major_concerns: Cannot integrate non-pharmacological modalities with pharmacological management into a coherent multimodal construct
Treats non-pharmacological modalities as interchangeable add-ons without mechanism-based patient matching
